title: Confidence-routed specialist ensemble with weighted conformal thresholding
summary: Selective prediction under shift shows calibrated confidence can be used to defer low-certainty cases while preserving stronger risk-coverage behavior, especially with weighted conformal calibration.
rationale: Selective prediction under shift shows calibrated confidence can be used to defer low-certainty cases while preserving stronger risk-coverage behavior, especially with weighted conformal calibration. In this competition, “defer” can mean route only uncertain rows to a specialist model instead of trusting one global expert.
implementation_hint: Train the current best global model plus one or two specialists on ambiguous regimes only, such as late-race old-tyre dry laps and wet/intermediate laps. Use strict OOF probabilities, temperature scaling, and a held-out calibration split to learn a confidence threshold; above threshold keep the base score, below threshold replace or blend with the specialist. Test weighted calibration by Year/regime to handle train-test drift.
expected_effect: Can improve hard slices without corrupting easy rows, giving a safer ensemble gain than globally reweighting all predictions.
risk: If specialists are not genuinely low-correlation, routing just adds noise. Calibration/routing must be learned out-of-fold or it will overstate gains.
